source.title: Accident Investigation Report for Public Release - Structural Damage, Crane, Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, South Marsh Island Block 130

actions_taken: Fire extinguished with 30# dry chemical extinguisher
event.summary: On February 4, 2023, at approximately 1405 hours, a small fire occurred on the thief hatch of the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank during welding and cutting operations for PAT-300 Water Injection Pump installation. Fire-retardant blankets failed to provide adequate protection, allowing slag to contact combustible vapors venting from the thief hatch. The flame was extinguished with no damage or injuries.

hazards.name: Pressurized oil tank with active vent lines

threats.name: Slag and sparks from welding/cutting operations contacting combustible vapors
Inadequate isolation of ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank allowing hydrocarbon ingress
Hydrocarbon vapors venting from thief hatch uncontrolled

consequences.name: Potential tank damage and personnel injury

controls.name: Fire-retardant blankets between work area and tank
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier to prevent ignition source contact with combustibles

supporting_text: Fire-retardant blankets were hung between the PAT-300 Water Injection Pump and the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank which were approximately 7 feet apart
the blankets were not located in a way that protected the thief hatch
The flame ignited due to the fire-retardant blankets failing to provide sufficient protection allowing slag to contact combustible vapers venting from the thief hatch

controls.name: Lockout/Tagout of ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank inlet and outlet valves
controls.barrier_role: Energy isolation to prevent hydrocarbon flow and pressure
controls.lod_basis: Procedural isolation per Talos Lockout/Tagout procedure 7.3.5

supporting_text: The JSA identified Energy Isolation and Lockout/Tagout but the lessee failed to lock or tag any valves associated with the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank
The ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank was isolated by closing all inlet valves from the NBK-300 Bulk Oil Treater but failed to lock and tag the valves as per the Talos Lockout / Tagout procedure - 7.3.5
The ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank outlet valves were not locked or tagged as per the Talos Lockout / Tagout procedure

controls.name: Installation of blind/skillet isolation on ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank
controls.barrier_role: Physical isolation of tank from pressurized system
controls.lod_basis: Preferred isolation method per Talos Lockout/Tagout procedure 7.3.5

supporting_text: There were no blinds/skillets installed during the welding operation nor was the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank isolated properly as per the mentioned policy

a single closed valve is not an acceptable isolation for potentially hazardous systems in the vicinity of the vessel entry and/or hot work, except when the facility/equipment is shut in and all pressures have been bled to atmospheric pressure. Insertion of blinds/skillets isolating the source is the preferred method

controls.name: Gas detection and monitoring near hot work area
controls.barrier_role: Detection of flammable atmosphere prior to and during hot work
controls.lod_basis: Hot Work Permit requirement for gas testing and continuous monitoring

supporting_text: The JSA for the operation contained checked off boxes labeled Hot Work which stated the following, 'I confirm flammable material have been removed or isolated', 'A gas test has been completed', and 'Gas will be monitored continuously'. The boxes were checked off, but according to personnel interviewed, gas readings were taken near the PAT-300 Water Injection Pump but had not been taken near the thief hatch on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank prior to hot work commencing and/or during the hot work operation

During the interview process, it was determined that gas detector readings were taken near the Water Injection Pump where welding operations were taking place but had not been taken prior to hot work commencing and/or during the hot work operation near the thief hatch

controls.name: Installation of tapped blind on ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank thief hatch
controls.barrier_role: Elimination of hydrocarbon vapor release point
controls.lod_basis: Mitigation identified by construction supervision prior to hot work

supporting_text: Construction supervision had previously identified a potential for hydrocarbon accumulation near the ABJ-310-Dry Oil Tank thief hatch. Mitigations were to include installation of a tapped blind installed on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank to replace the thief hatch and installing a hose to vent the hydrocarbon accumulation to a safe area. Neither of these mitigations were completed prior to the hot work commencing

controls.name: Installation of vent hose to direct hydrocarbon vapors to safe area
controls.barrier_role: Redirection of combustible vapors away from hot work area
controls.lod_basis: Mitigation identified by construction supervision prior to hot work

supporting_text: Mitigations were to include installation of a tapped blind installed on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank to replace the thief hatch and installing a hose to vent the hydrocarbon accumulation to a safe area. Neither of these mitigations were completed prior to the hot work commencing

controls.name: Fire watch personnel positioned at work area
controls.barrier_role: Early detection and response to fire ignition

supporting_text: the downstairs fire watch observed a flame coming from the thief hatch on the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank

There was a fire watch assigned on the west side of the well bay and a fire watch assigned on the production deck near the sump. Both mentioned areas were not in the immediate area where the work was being performed

controls.name: Dry chemical fire extinguisher availability and use

supporting_text: The fire was extinguished utilizing a 30# dry chemical extinguisher

controls.name: Isolation of ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank by closing inlet valves
controls.barrier_role: Partial isolation to reduce hydrocarbon inventory

supporting_text: The ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank was isolated by closing all inlet valves from the NBK-300 Bulk Oil Treater
a single closed valve is not an acceptable isolation for potentially hazardous systems in the vicinity of the vessel entry and/or hot work

controls.name: Reduction of oil level in ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank
controls.barrier_role: Reduction of hydrocarbon inventory and vapor generation

supporting_text: The oil level in the ABJ-310 Dry Oil Tank was dropped to approximately 200 barrels which has a 2000-barrel capacity

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
